Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Aamir Siddiqui
Message:
feat(rc6): /admin/pin accepts gen_pin_list.py replay-list schema (P1.3) (#79)

* feat(rc6): /admin/pin accepts replay-list manifest schema (P1.3)

Widens POST /admin/pin's deserializer via a serde(untagged) enum
so a single endpoint accepts both the existing strict shape AND
the replay-list manifest shape that pre-warm tooling naturally
emits. Resolves the workspace-memory P1 gap codified Apr 30
during the rep-0 cutover prep.

Wire shapes accepted:

  Strict (existing, pre-RC6 callers untouched):
    {key_hex, pool, [mv_name]}

  ReplaySingle (new):
    {bucket, key, etag, size_bytes, [pool], [offset], [length],
     [rg_ordinal], [mv_name]}

  ReplayBatch (new):
    [<ReplaySingle>, ...]      // capped at MAX_REPLAY_BATCH = 65536

Replay-shape entries are converted internally to strict-pin via
the canonical key_from_tuple(etag_bytes, offset, length, rg_ordinal)
sha256 — exact bit-equivalence with both `tools/gen_pin_list.py`
and the SHELF-24 file-driven loader, so a key admitted via the
replay path hits the same Foyer slot as one admitted via the
file loader or the strict POST.

Backward compat:
- Variant order is load-bearing (Strict tried first); pre-RC6
  callers bind to it bit-for-bit.
- Strict-form response shape unchanged (with one new optional
  `audit` field — empty string for strict callers, ignored by
  pre-RC6 clients per default-ignore-unknown JSON behaviour).
- New batch response carries {accepted, rejected, results: [...]}
  envelope only when the wire shape was an array; clients self-
  select into the new contract.

Implementation:
- New module shelfd/src/admin_pin_payload.rs (~470 LOC incl tests):
  PinPayload untagged enum, ReplayPinEntry struct, resolve()
  helper, pool_label and the etag quote-strip + sha256 derivation.
- http.rs admin_pin handler rewritten to dispatch through resolve()
  and emit either response shape based on the wire input.

Tests:
- 11 unit in admin_pin_payload (strict deserializes, replay-single
  deserializes, replay-batch deserializes, key parity replay-vs-
  strict, quoted-vs-unquoted etag parity, empty etag rejected,
  zero size rejected, unknown pool rejected, batch cap enforced,
  batch order preserved, strict-wins-on-overlap).
- 4 integration in tests/it_admin_pin_schema_flex (strict round-
  trips unchanged backward-compat lock-in, replay-single pins
  resident key, replay-batch returns per-entry results with both
  pinned and not_resident statuses, unknown pool returns 400).
- 371 existing shelfd lib tests + 8 it_admin tests still green
  (Strict variant continues to bind via untagged-enum order).

ADR: agents/out/adr/0023-rc6-admin-pin-replay-schema.md.

Reviewer notes:
- Untagged-enum variant order is documented as load-bearing in
  the module-level rustdoc and the ADR; do not reorder.
- The MAX_REPLAY_BATCH cap is the same value /cache/contains uses
  (65536); chunk client-side for larger pre-warm sets.
- If a future v2 schema lands (per-entry TTLs, priority hints),
  add a fourth variant with a discriminator field — see ADR-0023
  Alternatives D.
- DRAFT PR — orchestrator owns merge + image build + canary smoke.

Signed-off-by: aamirpw306 <aamir.k306@yahoo.com>

* chore(rc6): renumber ADR 0023 → 0026 (collision with #75)

Signed-off-by: aamirpw306 <aamir.k306@yahoo.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: aamirpw306 <aamir.k306@yahoo.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Aamir Siddiqui
Message:
feat(shelf-cost,shelfd): SHELF-40 dollars-saved counter (#68)

* feat(shelf-cost,shelfd): SHELF-40 dollars-saved counter

Introduces `crates/shelf-cost/` — a region-aware cost model that
turns shelf cache hits into integer-cents of S3 + EC2 + NAT spend
avoided — and wires `shelfd` to publish two new Prometheus series
on the existing `s3_shim` / `peer_fetch` hot paths via
`ReadOutcome`:

  * `shelf_s3_dollars_saved_total{region, outcome}` — IntCounterVec,
    unit "cents". `outcome ∈ {hit_memory, hit_disk, peer}`.
  * `shelf_s3_dollars_saved_rate_cents_per_sec{region, outcome}` —
    IntGaugeVec, 60 s rolling rate published by an in-process
    updater task so dashboards do not need
    `rate(... [60s]) * 0.01` everywhere.

Three downstream surfaces consume the same dollar formula —
`shelfd` (this PR), `shelf-advisor` (Tier-3), and the SHELF-37
Iceberg event-listener UDF. Centralising the formula in one
library prevents the three from drifting on coefficients, region
overrides, or rounding. Copying constants is review-blocking;
this PR enshrines that.

Every preset constant is doc-commented with the AWS pricing-page
URL it came from and an `AS_OF: YYYY-MM` date:

  * S3 GET (Standard, Tier-1) — https://aws.amazon.com/s3/pricing/
  * Cross-AZ data transfer    — https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/pricing/on-demand/
  * NAT-gateway data processing — https://aws.amazon.com/vpc/pricing/

Defaults match `us-east-1` and `ap-south-1` published rates as of
2026-04. The crate README carries a "stale price refresh"
runbook, and the design note at
`shelfd/docs/design-notes/SHELF-40-dollars-saved-counter.md`
documents the formula, the auditability story, and the
interaction with SHELF-37.

The first revision of the cost crate stored data-transfer rates
as `µ¢/byte`. That representation cannot hold any AWS
data-transfer rate without 100 %–1 075× rounding error: $0.01/GiB
is 0.000_931 µ¢/byte, which an `i64` `µ¢/byte` field can only
store as `0` (under-charge by 100 %) or `1` (over-charge by
1 075×). This PR moves storage to `µ¢/GiB`, which holds every
published rate exactly: $0.01/GiB = `1_000_000 µ¢/GiB`,
$0.045/GiB = `4_500_000 µ¢/GiB`, $0.00/GiB = `0 µ¢/GiB`. The
divide-by-`2^30` happens in `i128` inside `dollars_saved`, with
zero rounding residue on the coefficient table.

`shelfd::cost::CostState::observe` is called from
`s3_shim::handle_get_object` on every `ReadOutcome::Hit` and
from `peer_fetch::race` on every `RaceOutcome::PeerHit`. Both
paths bump the counter with the cents from
`CostModel::dollars_saved`; the `peer_az` is wired to
`DEFAULT_PEER_AZ = SameAz` (pessimistic) until SHELF-23
membership data feeds it. With the counter disabled
(`cache.cost.enabled: false`) the hot path short-circuits to a
single bool load.

  * Unit tests for every `HitEvent` variant; preset constants
    pinned literally to the published rates.
  * Property tests:
      - additive identity over arbitrary hit sequences (no float
        drift);
      - non-negativity on any non-negative coefficient table;
      - cross-AZ ≥ same-AZ for any byte count;
      - any negative coefficient via `CostConfig` rejected at
        load by `CostConfigError::NegativeCoefficient`.
  * Micro-benchmark `crates/shelf-cost/benches/dollars_saved_atomic_add.rs`
    for the < 5 ns/call wiring claim (criterion).
  * Integration test
    `shelfd/tests/it_dollars_saved.rs` (gated on
    `SHELF_INTEGRATION=1`) drives the SHELF-22 shim with 100 warm
    reads and asserts the counter advanced by **exactly** the
    cents the public formula predicts.
  * `shelfd::metrics` regression tests carry the two new series
    in `EXPOSED_SERIES`.

`charts/shelf/values.yaml` gains a `cache.cost.*` block (default
on, region us-east-1, every coefficient overrideable with units
documented in-line). `charts/shelf/templates/configmap-shelfd.yaml`
threads the block into `shelfd.yaml`.
`charts/shelf/grafana/dashboards/shelf-read-path.json` gains:

  * "S3 cost saved (cents/sec, last 60s)" timeseries panel,
    sum-by-`outcome` and total.
  * "S3 cost saved this month (running)" stat panel,
    `currencyUSD` rendered via `* 0.01`.

The penpencil overlay
(`infra/penpencil/charts/shelf/values-prod.yaml`) flips
`region: ap-south-1` and inherits the published Mumbai preset;
`natTraversalBasisPoints` stays `0` because the cluster reaches
S3 via a VPC gateway endpoint.

Refs: SHELF-40
Signed-off-by: aamirpw306 <aamir.k306@yahoo.com>
Made-with: Cursor

* chore: regen Cargo.lock after rebase onto rc.5 main

shelf-cost workspace version bumped rc.4 → rc.5 (tracks the workspace
version pin on main). No dependency-graph changes.

Signed-off-by: aamirpw306 <aamir.k306@yahoo.com>

  * S3 GET (Standard, Tier-1) — https://aws.amazon.com/s3/pricing/

(`cache.cost.enabled: false`) the hot path short-circuits to a
single bool load.

  * Unit tests for every `HitEvent` variant; preset constants
    pinned literally to the published rates.
  * Property tests:
      - additive identity over arbitrary hit sequences (no float
        drift);
      - non-negativity on any non-negative coefficient table;
      - cross-AZ ≥ same-AZ for any byte count;
      - any negative coefficient via `CostConfig` rejected at
        load by `CostConfigError::NegativeCoefficient`.
  * Micro-benchmark `crates/shelf-cost/benches/dollars_saved_atomic_add.rs`
    for the < 5 ns/call wiring claim (criterion).
  * Integration test
    `shelfd/tests/it_dollars_saved.rs` (gated on
    `SHELF_INTEGRATION=1`) drives the SHELF-22 shim with 100 warm
    reads and asserts the counter advanced by **exactly** the
    cents the public formula predicts.
  * `shelfd::metrics` regression tests carry the two new series
    in `EXPOSED_SERIES`.

`charts/shelf/values.yaml` gains a `cache.cost.*` block (default
on, region us-east-1, every coefficient overrideable with units
documented in-line). `charts/shelf/templates/configmap-shelfd.yaml`
threads the block into `shelfd.yaml`.
`charts/shelf/grafana/dashboards/shelf-read-path.json` gains:

  * "S3 cost saved (cents/sec, last 60s)" timeseries panel,
    sum-by-`outcome` and total.
  * "S3 cost saved this month (running)" stat panel,
    `currencyUSD` rendered via `* 0.01`.

The penpencil overlay
(`infra/penpencil/charts/shelf/values-prod.yaml`) flips
`region: ap-south-1` and inherits the published Mumbai preset;
`natTraversalBasisPoints` stays `0` because the cluster reaches
S3 via a VPC gateway endpoint.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: aamirpw306
Message:
feat(shelf-cost,shelfd): SHELF-40 dollars-saved counter

Introduces `crates/shelf-cost/` — a region-aware cost model that
turns shelf cache hits into integer-cents of S3 + EC2 + NAT spend
avoided — and wires `shelfd` to publish two new Prometheus series
on the existing `s3_shim` / `peer_fetch` hot paths via
`ReadOutcome`:

  * `shelf_s3_dollars_saved_total{region, outcome}` — IntCounterVec,
    unit "cents". `outcome ∈ {hit_memory, hit_disk, peer}`.
  * `shelf_s3_dollars_saved_rate_cents_per_sec{region, outcome}` —
    IntGaugeVec, 60 s rolling rate published by an in-process
    updater task so dashboards do not need
    `rate(... [60s]) * 0.01` everywhere.

## Why a separate crate

Three downstream surfaces consume the same dollar formula —
`shelfd` (this PR), `shelf-advisor` (Tier-3), and the SHELF-37
Iceberg event-listener UDF. Centralising the formula in one
library prevents the three from drifting on coefficients, region
overrides, or rounding. Copying constants is review-blocking;
this PR enshrines that.

## Coefficient citations

Every preset constant is doc-commented with the AWS pricing-page
URL it came from and an `AS_OF: YYYY-MM` date:

  * S3 GET (Standard, Tier-1) — https://aws.amazon.com/s3/pricing/
  * Cross-AZ data transfer    — https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/pricing/on-demand/
  * NAT-gateway data processing — https://aws.amazon.com/vpc/pricing/

Defaults match `us-east-1` and `ap-south-1` published rates as of
2026-04. The crate README carries a "stale price refresh"
runbook, and the design note at
`shelfd/docs/design-notes/SHELF-40-dollars-saved-counter.md`
documents the formula, the auditability story, and the
interaction with SHELF-37.

## Storage-unit fix (caught during this PR)

The first revision of the cost crate stored data-transfer rates
as `µ¢/byte`. That representation cannot hold any AWS
data-transfer rate without 100 %–1 075× rounding error: $0.01/GiB
is 0.000_931 µ¢/byte, which an `i64` `µ¢/byte` field can only
store as `0` (under-charge by 100 %) or `1` (over-charge by
1 075×). This PR moves storage to `µ¢/GiB`, which holds every
published rate exactly: $0.01/GiB = `1_000_000 µ¢/GiB`,
$0.045/GiB = `4_500_000 µ¢/GiB`, $0.00/GiB = `0 µ¢/GiB`. The
divide-by-`2^30` happens in `i128` inside `dollars_saved`, with
zero rounding residue on the coefficient table.

## Hot-path wiring

`shelfd::cost::CostState::observe` is called from
`s3_shim::handle_get_object` on every `ReadOutcome::Hit` and
from `peer_fetch::race` on every `RaceOutcome::PeerHit`. Both
paths bump the counter with the cents from
`CostModel::dollars_saved`; the `peer_az` is wired to
`DEFAULT_PEER_AZ = SameAz` (pessimistic) until SHELF-23
membership data feeds it. With the counter disabled
(`cache.cost.enabled: false`) the hot path short-circuits to a
single bool load.

## Tests

  * Unit tests for every `HitEvent` variant; preset constants
    pinned literally to the published rates.
  * Property tests:
      - additive identity over arbitrary hit sequences (no float
        drift);
      - non-negativity on any non-negative coefficient table;
      - cross-AZ ≥ same-AZ for any byte count;
      - any negative coefficient via `CostConfig` rejected at
        load by `CostConfigError::NegativeCoefficient`.
  * Micro-benchmark `crates/shelf-cost/benches/dollars_saved_atomic_add.rs`
    for the < 5 ns/call wiring claim (criterion).
  * Integration test
    `shelfd/tests/it_dollars_saved.rs` (gated on
    `SHELF_INTEGRATION=1`) drives the SHELF-22 shim with 100 warm
    reads and asserts the counter advanced by **exactly** the
    cents the public formula predicts.
  * `shelfd::metrics` regression tests carry the two new series
    in `EXPOSED_SERIES`.

## Helm + dashboard

`charts/shelf/values.yaml` gains a `cache.cost.*` block (default
on, region us-east-1, every coefficient overrideable with units
documented in-line). `charts/shelf/templates/configmap-shelfd.yaml`
threads the block into `shelfd.yaml`.
`charts/shelf/grafana/dashboards/shelf-read-path.json` gains:

  * "S3 cost saved (cents/sec, last 60s)" timeseries panel,
    sum-by-`outcome` and total.
  * "S3 cost saved this month (running)" stat panel,
    `currencyUSD` rendered via `* 0.01`.

The penpencil overlay
(`infra/penpencil/charts/shelf/values-prod.yaml`) flips
`region: ap-south-1` and inherits the published Mumbai preset;
`natTraversalBasisPoints` stays `0` because the cluster reaches
S3 via a VPC gateway endpoint.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: aamirpw306
Message:
plugin: SHELF-15 Parquet footer prefetch (async, fail-open)

Prime shelfd's metadata pool with the trailing N KiB of every
`.parquet` file Trino is about to open so the foreground read finds a
cache hit when the Iceberg reader eventually asks for the footer.
`io.shelf.client.FooterPrefetcher` wraps a 2-thread
`ThreadPoolExecutor` with `CallerRunsPolicy` (never OOM, never block
the caller) and issues async `rangeGet(Pool.METADATA, ...)` through
the existing `ShelfHttpClient`. Trigger lives in
`ShelfFileSystem.newInputFile(Location)` and fires only when
`enabled && prefetch.enabled && path ends with .parquet (case-insensitive)
&& resolver.ownerFor(key) resolves a target`.

Pool selection is deliberate: body reads still route `.parquet →
ROWGROUP` via the existing `poolFor`; only the footer window routes to
`METADATA` via the new `poolForFooter()`. A single file therefore has
bytes in both pools by design. New config key `shelf.footer.prefetch.kib`
(default 64, min 1, max 256) is parsed in `ShelfConfig.fromMap` with
full validation + negative-path tests. Small-file edge clamps the
request window to `[0, length)`. A plugin-side `PrefetchMetrics` seam
(`AtomicLong` counters) is exposed via `ShelfFileSystem.prefetchMetrics()`
for test observation without pulling in `micrometer`.

Fail-open invariant preserved: prefetch failures never surface to
Trino. A `Throwable` boundary at the executor task swallows even OOM
to a FINE log. Verified in `FooterPrefetcherTest` (5 cases: happy
path, unreachable client, small-file clamp, disabled config,
completion future visible to tests). `ShelfFileSystemTest` gains
matching cases covering `.parquet` invocation, `.orc` non-invocation,
and empty-ring skip. `mvn test` reports 94 green (+16). Mark
SHELF-08 and SHELF-15 closed in `agents/out/03-plan.md`;
SHELF-15's end-to-end hit-ratio conformance defers to SHELF-12's
docker-compose harness. Design notes at
`clients/trino/docs/design-notes/SHELF-15-footer-prefetch.md`.
